summaryText: Pastor Todd Baffo continued Impact Church's series on identity by exploring the call to spiritual maturity from 1 Corinthians 13:11. He explained that many believers struggle with 'masks'—patterns of insecurity, negative self-talk, and false beliefs—that prevent them from living authentically as God created them. The message centered on three fundamental areas that need to mature: our speech, our understanding, and our thought life. Pastor Todd emphasized that our words have creative power, for good or ill, and we must learn to speak life and faith rather than fear and poison. He taught that our understanding of situations must be based on God's truth, not our own fearful or childish reasoning. Finally, he challenged the congregation to build a rock-solid, healthy thought life by resisting negative patterns and drawing near to God. The sermon concluded with a powerful reminder that what God says about us matters more than our own thoughts or the opinions of others, and that through Christ, we have the authority to put away childish things and step into our God-given purpose.
mainPoints: Our speech has the power to create; we must use our words to sow seeds of faith and life, not fear and poison.
Our understanding must be based on God's truth, not on our own fearful, childish, or fictional reasoning.
We must build a rock-solid, healthy thought life by resisting negative patterns and drawing near to God.

versesMentioned.text: When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I put the ways of childhood behind me.
What goes into someone’s mouth does not defile them, but what comes out of their mouth, that is what defiles them.
From the fruit of their lips people are filled with good things, and the work of their hands brings them reward.
Beat your plowshares into swords and your pruning hooks into spears. Let the weakling say, ‘I am strong!’
Truly I tell you, if anyone says to this mountain, ‘Go, throw yourself into the sea,’ and does not doubt in their heart but believes that what they say will happen, it will be done for them.
The one who gets wisdom loves life; the one who cherishes understanding will soon prosper.
You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you might go and bear fruit—fruit that will last—and so that whatever you ask in my name the Father will give you.
I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made; your works are wonderful, I know that full well.
For the Spirit God gave us does not make us timid, but gives us power, love and self-discipline.
Submit yourselves, then, to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. Come near to God and he will come near to you. Wash your hands, you sinners, and purify your hearts, you double-minded.

Monday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:11 (NIV) - 'When I became a man, I put the ways of childhood behind me.'
Monday.focus: Identify one 'childish' pattern in your speech, like negative self-talk or complaining.
Monday.action: Catch yourself in one moment of negative speech today and consciously replace it with a statement of faith or gratitude.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me become aware of my words. Give me the courage to put away childish speech and speak life today.

Tuesday.scripture: Proverbs 19:8 (NIV) - 'The one who cherishes understanding will soon prosper.'
Tuesday.focus: Examine your understanding of a current challenge.
Tuesday.action: Write down your initial understanding of a difficult situation. Then, prayerfully ask God for His perspective based on His truth.
Tuesday.prayer: God, I submit my understanding to You. Help me see this situation through Your eyes of truth, not my own fear or assumptions.

Wednesday.scripture: 1 Timothy 1:7 (NIV) - 'For the Spirit God gave us does not make us timid, but gives us power, love and self-discipline.'
Wednesday.focus: Resist a fearful or anxious thought.
Wednesday.action: When a worrying thought arises, verbally reject it by saying, 'I resist that thought in Jesus' name,' and replace it with a scripture about God's power.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, thank you for power, love, and self-discipline. I draw on Your strength to resist fearful thoughts and stand firm in Your truth.

Thursday.scripture: Psalm 139:14 (NIV) - 'I am fearfully and wonderfully made; your works are wonderful, I know that full well.'
Thursday.focus: Renew your mind with God's truth about your identity.
Thursday.action: Look in the mirror and declare this verse out loud three times. Let the truth of being God's wonderful creation sink in.
Thursday.prayer: Father, I praise You for how You made me. Help me to 'know full well' in my heart that I am Your wonderful workmanship.

Friday.scripture: James 4:7-8 (NIV) - 'Submit yourselves... to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you. Come near to God and he will come near to you.'
Friday.focus: Practice the two-step process: submit and draw near.
Friday.action: Spend 5 extra minutes in quiet prayer today, simply submitting your day to God and asking Him to draw near to you.
Friday.prayer: Lord, I submit my will, my plans, and my struggles to You today. As I draw near, thank You for Your promise to draw near to me.

Saturday.scripture: Mark 11:23 (NIV) - 'If anyone says to this mountain, ‘Go, throw yourself into the sea,’ and does not doubt... it will be done for them.'
Saturday.action: Identify one persistent obstacle in your life (a habit, a fear, a relational strain). Speak directly to it in prayer, commanding it to move in Jesus' name, based on God's power, not your own.
Saturday.prayer: Jesus, I believe in Your authority. I speak to [name the obstacle] and command it to move. My trust is in Your power, not my own. Thank you for the victory.
